Best Sri Lanka Tour Package from India – Discover the Island of Serene Beauty and Culture

Discover the best Sri Lanka tour package from India, where lush tea gardens, golden beaches, ancient temples, and exotic wildlife come together in perfect harmony. With Travelxploria, your trusted Best Travel Agency and experienced International Tour Operator, explore Sri Lanka with expertly curated packages tailored for honeymooners, families, and group travelers from India. For more diverse travel options, check out our wide range of Tour Package India.
Curated by our certified travel consultants with over a decade of experience in South Asia tourism.
Whether you're seeking a SriLanka tour package from Chennai, a relaxing SriLanka tour package from Bangalore, or a scenic SriLanka tour package from Kochi, we have the perfect itinerary waiting for you.

Why Choose a Sri Lanka Tour Package from India?

Proximity, Visa-Free Entry, and Affordable Travel
Sri Lanka is just a short flight away from major Indian cities and offers visa-free entry for Indian citizens (source). It’s an ideal destination for a quick international getaway that won’t break the bank—especially with our
SriLanka tour package from Kerala, Mumbai, or Kolkata.
Pro Tip: Flights from Chennai to Colombo take under 90 minutes, making Sri Lanka one of the most accessible international destinations for Indian travelers.
Rich Heritage, Pristine Beaches, and Scenic Hill Stations
The country boasts a stunning mix of cultural treasures, serene beaches, and misty hills. With our best Sri Lanka tour package from India, you'll explore ancient temples, unwind on palm-fringed shores, and trek through the cool mountain air in tea country.
Must-see heritage sites include the Sigiriya Rock Fortress and the Sacred City of Anuradhapura.
Perfect for Honeymooners, Families, and Solo Explorers
Whether it’s a SriLanka tour package for couple, a family Sri Lanka tour package from India, or a solo escape, our itineraries suit every kind of traveler. You’ll find adventure, relaxation, and cultural immersion all in one trip. For honeymooners, explore our special honeymoon packages and for solo travelers, see our solo travel packages.

Top Sri Lanka Tour Packages from India
5 to 7 Days Sri Lanka Tour Package from India – Kandy, Colombo & Nuwara Eliya
Experience the highlights of Sri Lanka in under a week. Start in Colombo, visit the sacred city of Kandy, and enjoy the British colonial charm of Nuwara Eliya—the perfect SriLanka tour package from Kolkata or Chennai.
Sample Itinerary:
Day 1: Arrive Colombo – City Tour
Day 2: Transfer to Kandy – Visit Temple of the Tooth
Day 3: Kandy to Nuwara Eliya – Tea Estate Visit
Day 4: Ella Trekking – Scenic Train Ride
Day 5: Colombo Shopping + Departure
Sri Lanka Honeymoon Packages for Indian Couples
Our romantic SriLanka tour package for couple features candle-lit dinners, beachside resorts, and serene hill stations. Book a Sri Lanka honeymoon package from India at best price, designed with privacy, luxury, and unforgettable experiences.
Resort Recommendation: Jetwing Lighthouse in Galle or 98 Acres in Ella are ideal for honeymooners.

Budget-Friendly Sri Lanka Holiday Packages from India
Travel affordably without compromising on quality. Our packages—from SriLanka tour package from Mumbai to Kochi—include flights, transfers, and curated stays, all at competitive rates.
Tip: Book during the shoulder seasons (April or October) for the lowest prices and fewer crowds.
Customized Sri Lanka Itineraries for Families and Small Groups
From wildlife safaris to cultural tours, we offer personalized plans perfect for a family Sri Lanka tour package from India or guided Sri Lanka group tour packages from India with multilingual guides and flexible options.
Customization includes dietary preferences, elderly-friendly itineraries, and activity adjustments for kids. For more family travel options, see our dedicated family tour packages.

Must-Visit Places in Sri Lanka Covered in Tour Packages

Colombo – Colonial Architecture, Shopping & Nightlife
Begin in Colombo, the vibrant capital that fuses modern malls and colonial-era buildings. Enjoy local cuisine, street markets, and a buzzing nightlife. Don’t miss Pettah Market and the Dutch Hospital Precinct.

Kandy – Temple of the Tooth and Cultural Shows
Kandy, Sri Lanka’s cultural hub, is home to the Temple of the Tooth Relic and traditional Kandyan dance performances. Don’t miss the Esala Perahera festival if you visit in July-August.

Nuwara Eliya – Tea Gardens and Mountain Views
Nicknamed “Little England,” this highland retreat offers lush tea plantations, cool weather, and scenic train rides—an ideal stop for nature lovers and honeymooners. Recommended: Visit Pedro Tea Estate and Gregory Lake.

Bentota – Beach Resorts and Water Sports
Located on the southwestern coast, Bentota is perfect for water sports, beach lounging, or romantic stays by the sea—ideal for couples booking a SriLanka tour package for couple.

Sigiriya & Dambulla – Ancient Rock Fortresses and Temples
Explore Sri Lanka’s ancient past at Sigiriya, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and the cave temples of Dambulla, rich with murals and Buddha statues. Tip: Climb early in the morning to avoid crowds and heat.

Inclusions in the Best Sri Lanka Tour Packages from India
Return Flights from Major Indian Cities
We offer all-inclusive packages with return airfare from Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai, Kolkata, Kerala, Kochi, and Bangalore. You can also explore other nearby destinations with our nepal tour package or broaden your trip with the norway tour package from india.

Hotel Accommodation with Daily Breakfast
Enjoy premium or boutique stays, including breakfast and other amenities tailored to your package. Hotel categories available: 3-star to 5-star and boutique eco-stays.

Private Transfers and Local Sightseeing Tours
Every best Sri Lanka tour package from India includes AC vehicle transfers, licensed guides, and curated sightseeing to key attractions.

Travel Insurance and Indian Language Tour Assistance (Optional)
Optional Indian-language support and travel insurance ensure a smooth and secure experience for families, couples, and groups.

Best Time to Visit Sri Lanka from India

December to April – Ideal Weather for the West and South Coast
Travel during this period to enjoy the sunshine and coastal escapes of Bentota, Mirissa, and Colombo.

May to September – Best for East Coast Beaches
Enjoy the calm beaches of Trincomalee and Arugam Bay with less rainfall and vibrant marine life.

Festival Seasons – Esala Perahera and Sinhala New Year
Time your trip around April (Sinhala New Year) or July-August (Kandy's Esala Perahera) to witness Sri Lanka's vibrant traditions.

Frequently Asked Questions About Sri Lanka Tour Packages from India

Do Indian citizens need a visa to visit Sri Lanka?
No. As of now, Indian passport holders can enter Sri Lanka visa-free (verify here), making it easier than ever to plan a quick international holiday.

What is the ideal duration for a Sri Lanka trip from India?
A 5 to 7-day itinerary is ideal to explore key cities like Colombo, Kandy, Nuwara Eliya, and Bentota. You can also extend your stay for wildlife safaris or deeper cultural immersion.

Is Sri Lanka a good honeymoon destination for Indian couples?
Absolutely. The Sri Lanka honeymoon package from India at best price includes romantic stays, private beach dinners, scenic train rides, and spa treatments designed for couples.

What are some top options for family-friendly Sri Lanka tours?
Our family Sri Lanka tour package from India offers kid-friendly hotels, guided nature tours, and cultural experiences like elephant spotting and temple visits. Add-on: Optional family rooms, vegetarian meal plans, and educational cultural stops.

Are there group tours available for Sri Lanka from India?
Yes, our guided Sri Lanka group tour packages from India are perfect for friends, senior travelers, or corporate retreats, offering shared experiences with expert local guides. Features: Group leader, shared coach transport, and fixed departure dates.

How to Book the Best Sri Lanka Tour Package from India

 Trusted Indian Travel Agencies and Online Booking Sites
Book your trip via Travelxploria, recognized as one of the Best Tour Operators in India, known for curated itineraries, transparency, and value. Check customer reviews and testimonials on Google & Trustpilot.

 Tips for Choosing the Right Sri Lanka Itinerary
Choose based on your travel style—beach, heritage, wildlife, or a mix. Consider the weather and duration when selecting from SriLanka tour package from Bangalore, Mumbai, or Kochi.

 What to Pack and Know Before You Travel
Pack light clothes, sun protection, modest attire for temples, and your passport. Respect local customs and try local dishes like hoppers and kottu roti for an authentic experience. Travel Tip: Carry a universal adapter, sunscreen, mosquito repellent, and Sri Lankan Rupees.
Explore Sri Lanka with Travelxploria—your journey to paradise starts with the best Sri Lanka tour package from India.

packages.overview:
• Arrive in Sri Lanka, meet Travelxploria representative, and transfer to the beautiful coastal town of Negombo for a relaxing evening.

  • Visit the Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage en route, explore the sacred Temple of the Tooth, and enjoy a vibrant Kandyan cultural show.

  • Discover panoramic views from Kandy View Point, visit Ramboda Falls, Hanuman Temple, and relax by Gregory Lake in Nuwara Eliya.

  • Enjoy scenic waterfalls, optional white water rafting at Kitulgala, and head to the golden beaches of Hikkaduwa.

  • Embark on a Madu River Boat tour, explore Colombo’s dynamic shopping scene, and relax by Mount Lavinia Beach.

  • Enjoy final sightseeing or shopping in Colombo before departing with wonderful memories of Sri Lanka.

Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka, with a fascinating stop at a spice garden in Matale along the way. Learn about Sri Lanka’s world-famous spices, which have been treasured for centuries and remain a celebrated export today.

In the evening, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya, located just 5.5 km west of Kandy near the banks of the Mahaweli River. Renowned for its lush landscapes and impressive collection of orchids, the garden attracts over a million visitors each year.

At 4:45 PM, experience the spirit of Sri Lanka with a Cultural Dance Show, where traditional music, vibrant costumes, and age-old dance rituals come alive in an enthralling performance.

After the show, proceed to your hotel in Kandy for check-in and relaxation.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kandy.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

A special highlight of Hikkaduwa is the chance to interact with baby turtles, which can often be seen along the public beach. These gentle creatures occasionally come close to shore, where locals and visitors alike can feed them small bits of food—a truly heartwarming and unforgettable experience for nature lovers and families.

Dinner and overnight stay in Hikkaduwa.

As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

packages.overview:
• ​Arrival in Sri Lanka, transfer to Negombo for a relaxing day at the beach, seafood, and local exploration.
  • Visit Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage, Temple of the Tooth, and enjoy a traditional Kandyan dance performance.
  • ​Explore Kandy’s Viewpoint, Ramboda Waterfall, Hanuman Temple, and Gregory Lake, with an optional boat or jet ski ride.
  • ​Scenic drive to Hikkaduwa, with stops at Devon and St. Clair's Falls, optional white water rafting in Kitulgala.
  • Enjoy a Madu River boat tour, followed by a visit to Colombo’s shopping and cultural landmarks.
  • ​Last-minute shopping or sightseeing in Colombo before your departure.​

Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka, with a fascinating stop at a spice garden in Matale along the way. Learn about Sri Lanka’s world-famous spices, which have been treasured for centuries and remain a celebrated export today.

In the evening, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya, located just 5.5 km west of Kandy near the banks of the Mahaweli River. Renowned for its lush landscapes and impressive collection of orchids, the garden attracts over a million visitors each year.

At 4:45 PM, experience the spirit of Sri Lanka with a Cultural Dance Show, where traditional music, vibrant costumes, and age-old dance rituals come alive in an enthralling performance.

After the show, proceed to your hotel in Kandy for check-in and relaxation.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kandy.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

packages.overview:
• Arrival at Katunayake Airport, visit Munnewaram and Manawari Hindu Temples in Chillaw, then proceed to Sigiriya for an overnight stay after visiting Dambulla Golden Temple.
  • Climb Sigiriya Rock Fortress, visit a Spice Garden, explore the Temple of the Tooth, and enjoy a cultural show in Kandy.
  • Visit the Temple of the Tooth and Kandy View Point, then journey to Nuwara Eliya, stopping at Ramboda Waterfall and Hanuman Temple, with a boat ride option at Gregory Lake.
  • Travel to Ella, visit the Sita Amman Temple, and explore the Nine Arch Bridge and Ella’s scenic landscapes.
  • Visit the Murugan Temple in Kataragama, a significant Ramayana site, and explore its spiritual surroundings.
  • Stop at Ussangoda Ramayana Site, visit Rumassala Sanjeevani Mountain, explore Galle Dutch Fort, and relax at Hikkaduwa Beach.
  • Enjoy a boat ride on the Hikkaduwa River, visit Pancha Muga Anjaneyer Temple, and explore Colombo with shopping, a city tour, and visits to cultural landmarks.
  • Last-minute shopping or sightseeing in Colombo before departure, concluding the tour with heartfelt gratitude from Supreme Travels.

Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka, with a fascinating stop at a spice garden in Matale along the way. Learn about Sri Lanka’s world-famous spices, which have been treasured for centuries and remain a celebrated export today.

In the evening, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya, located just 5.5 km west of Kandy near the banks of the Mahaweli River. Renowned for its lush landscapes and impressive collection of orchids, the garden attracts over a million visitors each year.

At 4:45 PM, experience the spirit of Sri Lanka with a Cultural Dance Show, where traditional music, vibrant costumes, and age-old dance rituals come alive in an enthralling performance.

After the show, proceed to your hotel in Kandy for check-in and relaxation.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kandy.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

Along the way, visit the sacred Seetha Amman Temple, located in the village of Seetha Eliya, just 1 km from the Hakgala Botanical Garden. According to the Ramayana epic, this is believed to be the place where Seetha was held captive by King Ravana, and today the temple stands as a place of devotion and historical significance.

Continue onwards to Ella, a serene hill country village nestled among lush greenery, misty mountains, and tea plantations. Known for its cool climate and laid-back charm, Ella is a haven for hikers and nature lovers alike.

Explore the highlights of Ella, including a visit to the iconic Nine Arch Bridge, a marvel of colonial-era railway construction set amidst dense jungle. Embark on a short hike to Little Adam’s Peak for panoramic views of the valley, and enjoy a relaxed Ella city tour, taking in the town’s cozy cafés, markets, and tranquil surroundings.

Dinner and overnight stay in Ella, surrounded by its peaceful, picture-perfect landscapes.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

packages.overview:
• Arrive in Sri Lanka and travel to Sigiriya, with an enroute visit to Dambulla Golden Temple and Sigiriya Rock Fortress.
  • Drive to Trincomalee, visit Koneswaran Temple, Kanniya Hot Springs, and Marble Beach.
  • Travel to Kandy, visit a spice garden, the Temple of the Tooth, and enjoy a cultural show.
  • Explore Kandy, visit the Kandy View Point, Ramboda Waterfall, Hanuman Temple, and Gregory Lake before heading to Nuwara Eliya.
  • Visit Sita Amman Temple, Nine Arch Bridge, Little Adams Peak, and explore Ella.
  • Travel to Kataragama, visit Ravana Waterfall, and the Murugan Temple.
  • Proceed to Hikkaduwa via Galle Dutch Fort and enjoy the beach and turtle feeding in Hikkaduwa.
  • Take a Madu River safari, visit Colombo’s landmarks and markets.
  • Final sightseeing or shopping in Colombo before departure.

Welcome to Sri Lanka!

Greetings from Travelxploria!

Embark on an unforgettable 8-night, 9-day journey to explore the enchanting beauty and vibrant culture of Sri Lanka. Your adventure begins the moment you arrive. Upon arrival at Katunayake Airport, our Travelxploria representative will be there to warmly welcome you and assist you throughout your trip.

Your journey starts with a scenic drive to Sigiriya, a journey of approximately 3 to 3.5 hours through the stunning Sri Lankan countryside. En route, visit the renowned Dambulla Golden Temple, also known as the Dambulla Cave Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage site. This impressive temple complex, one of the largest and best-preserved in Sri Lanka, showcases a beautiful collection of Buddhist art and statues.

Enjoy lunch in Dambulla before continuing on to Sigiriya Rock. Sigiriya, an ancient rock fortress perched 200 meters above the ground, is famous for its breathtaking beauty and historical importance. Built in the 5th century by King Kasyapa, this UNESCO World Heritage site offers visitors a chance to explore the remnants of palaces, frescoes, and stunning water gardens. Don't miss the Lion’s Gate, a grand entrance with massive lion paws carved into stone, and the panoramic views that await at the summit.

In the evening, check in to your hotel in Sigiriya, where you can relax and unwind after your day of exploration. If time allows, enjoy a leisurely stroll around the hotel or take in the serene surroundings.

Overnight stay in Sigiriya.

After enjoying a hearty breakfast, set off for the beautiful coastal city of Trincomalee, rich in history, culture, and scenic beauty. Your first stop is the iconic Koneswaram Temple, perched atop a cliff overlooking the Indian Ocean. This ancient shrine, dedicated to Lord Shiva, is believed to be the place where Ravana once worshipped Shiva to gain his divine powers—offering not just spiritual significance but also breathtaking ocean views.

En route, stop at the Kanniya Hot Springs, a cluster of natural thermal wells with warm waters said to have healing properties. These springs offer a soothing experience, perfect for relaxing and recharging during your journey.

Next, head to the tranquil Marble Beach, known for its soft white sands, crystal-clear waters, and the smooth, marble-like stones that line its shores. Though not directly connected to the Ramayana, this serene setting invites reflection and relaxation, making it an ideal spot to unwind.

In the evening, arrive in Trincomalee and check in to your hotel.Dinner and overnight stay in Trincomalee.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

After breakfast, begin your scenic journey to Ella, one of Sri Lanka’s most enchanting highland destinations. Along the way, visit the sacred Seetha Amman Temple, located in the village of Seetha Eliya, just 1 km from the Hakgala Botanical Garden. According to the Ramayana epic, this is believed to be the place where Seetha was held captive by King Ravana, and today the temple stands as a place of devotion and historical significance.

Continue onwards to Ella, a serene hill country village nestled among lush greenery, misty mountains, and tea plantations. Known for its cool climate and laid-back charm, Ella is a haven for hikers and nature lovers alike.

Explore the highlights of Ella, including a visit to the iconic Nine Arch Bridge, a marvel of colonial-era railway construction set amidst dense jungle. Embark on a short hike to Little Adam’s Peak for panoramic views of the valley, and enjoy a relaxed Ella city tour, taking in the town’s cozy cafés, markets, and tranquil surroundings.

Dinner and overnight stay in Ella, surrounded by its peaceful, picture-perfect landscapes.

After breakfast in Ella, embark on a scenic drive to Kataragama, a journey of approximately 3 to 4 hours through Sri Lanka’s stunning southern landscapes. The route winds through tea plantations, lush forests, and rolling hills, offering breathtaking views along the way.

En route, make a stop at the enchanting Ravana Waterfall, one of Sri Lanka’s most beautiful and legendary cascades. Tied to the Ramayana epic, this waterfall is not only a natural wonder but also a site of mythological significance.

Upon arrival in Kataragama, visit the revered Murugan Temple, one of the island’s most important spiritual sites. Dedicated to Lord Murugan (also known as Kartikeya), the Hindu god of war and the son of Lord Shiva and Parvati, the temple draws devotees of multiple faiths, making it a powerful symbol of religious harmony.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kataragama.

Hikkaduwa is a charming coastal town on Sri Lanka’s southern shore, renowned for its golden beaches, vibrant coral reefs, and relaxed, beachside vibe. Its crystal-clear waters make it an ideal destination for snorkeling, diving, and soaking up the tropical sunshine.

A special highlight of Hikkaduwa is the chance to interact with baby turtles, which can often be seen along the public beach. These gentle creatures occasionally come close to shore, where locals and visitors alike can feed them small bits of food—a truly heartwarming and unforgettable experience for nature lovers and families.

Dinner and overnight stay in Hikkaduwa.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

packages.overview:
• ​Arrive in Sri Lanka, visit Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage, and explore Dambulla Cave Temple en route to Sigiriya.
  • ​Discover the Sigiriya Rock Fortress, visit a spice garden, and enjoy a cultural show in Kandy.
  • Explore Kandy’s highlights, then travel to Nuwara Eliya with scenic stops at waterfalls and tea plantations.
  • Take a train to Ella, explore its natural gems, and head to Yala via Kataragama Temple.
  • Embark on a thrilling Yala safari, then visit historic Galle Fort on the way to Bentota.
  • Enjoy a river safari, turtle hatchery visit, and water sports along Bentota’s coast.
  • Explore Colombo’s vibrant city life, shop, and relax by the Indian Ocean sunset.
  • Depart from Colombo with unforgettable memories of your Sri Lankan journey.​

Upon your arrival in Sri Lanka, you will be warmly welcomed by our Travelxploria representative at the airport. From there, begin your journey to Sigiriya with an en route visit to the renowned Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage. This beloved sanctuary provides a safe haven for abandoned and injured elephants, where both adults and calves thrive under dedicated care. Witness the elephants during their feeding and bathing times, which offer unforgettable moments to see these gentle giants up close. The orphanage also operates a successful captive breeding program, making it a must-visit attraction for both tourists and locals alike.

Visit the iconic Dambulla Cave Temple, also known as the Golden Temple of Dambulla—a UNESCO World Heritage Site located in central Sri Lanka. This remarkable site is the largest and best-preserved cave temple complex in the country, featuring stunning Buddha statues and intricate murals that date back centuries.

After the visit, proceed to Sigiriya, check in to your hotel, and unwind after a day of exploration.

Dinner and overnight stay in Sigiriya.

After breakfast, embark on a journey to discover the 8th Wonder of the World – the Sigiriya Rock Fortress, also known as Ravana’s Palace. Dating back to the 5th century A.D., this awe-inspiring fortress perched atop a massive rock outcrop served as the royal citadel of King Kassapa for over 18 years. A marvel of ancient artistry and engineering, Sigiriya captivates visitors with its stunning architecture, landscaped gardens, and intricate frescoes—testaments to a rich heritage that continues to impress all who witness its grandeur.

Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka, with a fascinating stop at a spice garden in Matale along the way. Learn about Sri Lanka’s world-famous spices, which have been treasured for centuries and remain a celebrated export today.

In the evening, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya, located just 5.5 km west of Kandy near the banks of the Mahaweli River. Renowned for its lush landscapes and impressive collection of orchids, the garden attracts over a million visitors each year.

At 4:45 PM, experience the spirit of Sri Lanka with a Cultural Dance Show, where traditional music, vibrant costumes, and age-old dance rituals come alive in an enthralling performance.

After the show, proceed to your hotel in Kandy for check-in and relaxation.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kandy.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

After breakfast, proceed to Nanu Oya Railway Station for an optional train ride to Ella, enjoying the scenic beauty of Sri Lanka's hill country.

Upon arrival in Ella, explore some of its most iconic landmarks, including the Nine Arch Bridge, Little Adams Peak, and Ravana Falls. Ella, nestled in the heart of the island's lush highlands, is known for its cool climate and stunning views. Steeped in the legend of Rama and Sita from the Ramayana, many of its natural attractions are linked to the mythological tale, with landmarks named after King Ravana, the local deity. Ravana Ella Falls, cascading dramatically, is a highlight, along with the towering Ella Rock and the breathtaking views from Ella Gap, which offers panoramic vistas all the way to the southern coast.

After exploring Ella, continue your journey to Yala, stopping en route to visit the Katharagama Temple, a significant spiritual site.

Check in to your hotel in Yala, relax, and enjoy your evening.

Dinner & Overnight stay in Yala.

Begin your day with an exciting wildlife safari at Yala National Park (from 5:30 AM to 9:30 AM). As the second-largest national park in Sri Lanka, Yala is renowned for having the highest concentration of leopards in the world. The park’s vast landscape is a mix of scrub jungle, rocky outcrops, and salt and freshwater lagoons. Home to elephants, leopards, sloth bears, crocodiles, wild buffalo, and sambur, Yala also features herds of spotted deer, wild boars, langur and macaque monkeys, and various small mammals. Over 130 species of birds, including the peacock, are found here, and the coastal areas also serve as a nesting ground for marine turtles. After an adventurous morning, return to your hotel for breakfast.

Next, proceed to Bentota, stopping en route to visit the historic Galle Fort. Spend some time exploring this heritage city, which has stood resilient for centuries against invasions from colonial powers and the elements. Originally built by the Portuguese, the famous Dutch Fort was fortified by the Dutch and later preserved by the British. The fort, battered by the ocean for over 400 years, withstood even the force of a tsunami, and today, it remains a charming attraction, preserving its colonial architecture and offering a glimpse into the past.

Afterward, continue to Bentota for check-in at your hotel and relax.

Dinner & Overnight stay in Bentota.

​

After breakfast, set out to explore the scenic coastal city of Bentota, located on the southern shores of Sri Lanka. Known for its natural beauty and birdlife, Bentota is a haven for nature lovers, with opportunities to spot rare species such as the Indian Heron and various migratory birds along the riverbanks.

Begin your day with a Madu River boat ride, a serene journey through lush mangroves and vibrant wetlands. This ecological treasure is home to a wide variety of flora and fauna, including 70 species of fish, 50 types of butterflies, and over 100 species of birds—making it a paradise for wildlife and bird enthusiasts.

Next, visit the Kosgoda Turtle Hatchery, a vital conservation project dedicated to protecting Sri Lanka’s endangered sea turtles. Learn about the life cycle of turtles and witness the heartwarming sight of baby turtles being cared for before they are released into the sea.

In the evening, enjoy some thrilling water sports activities in Bentota, such as jet skiing, banana boat rides, or windsurfing, perfect for adventure seekers.

Dinner and overnight stay in Bentota.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

packages.overview:
• Warm welcome at the airport followed by en route visits to a spice garden and Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage before reaching Kandy.
  • Full-day exploration of Kandy’s cultural treasures including the Temple of the Tooth, Royal Botanical Gardens, and a traditional cultural dance show.
  • Scenic drive to Nuwara Eliya with stops at Ramboda Waterfalls, Hanuman Temple, and visits to tea plantations and colonial landmarks.
  • Discover Ella’s natural wonders like Ravana Falls and Nine Arch Bridge before heading to Yala for an overnight stay.
  • Morning departure to Mirissa for leisure time on serene beaches and optional whale-watching adventures.
  • Explore the historic Galle Dutch Fort and lighthouse before enjoying the beaches and water sports in Bentota.
  • Return to Colombo for a city tour covering iconic sites like Galle Face, Pettah Bazaar, Colombo Museum, and the Lotus Tower.
  • After breakfast, transfer to the airport with cherished memories of your Sri Lankan adventure.

Upon your arrival at Colombo Bandaranaike International Airport, you will be warmly welcomed by our Travelxploria representative. After a brief introduction, you will be escorted to your comfortable vehicle to begin your exciting holiday in Sri Lanka.

Your first destination will be the beautiful city of Kandy.

En route, enjoy a complimentary visit to a Spice Garden, where you can discover the rich aromas and flavors of Sri Lankan spices.

You will also visit the renowned Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage, a sanctuary that cares for orphaned and injured wild elephants. Located in the village of Pinnawala, this facility hosts the largest herd of captive elephants in the world and has been a vital part of conservation efforts since its establishment in 1975 by the Sri Lanka Department of Wildlife Conservation.

After an enriching day of sightseeing, proceed to Kandy for check-in.

Overnight stay at your hotel in Kandy.

Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka, with a fascinating stop at a spice garden in Matale along the way. Learn about Sri Lanka’s world-famous spices, which have been treasured for centuries and remain a celebrated export today.

In the evening, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya, located just 5.5 km west of Kandy near the banks of the Mahaweli River. Renowned for its lush landscapes and impressive collection of orchids, the garden attracts over a million visitors each year.

At 4:45 PM, experience the spirit of Sri Lanka with a Cultural Dance Show, where traditional music, vibrant costumes, and age-old dance rituals come alive in an enthralling performance.

After the show, proceed to your hotel in Kandy for check-in and relaxation.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kandy.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

After breakfast at the hotel, head to Mirissa, a picturesque town on Sri Lanka’s south coast in the Matara District. Located approximately 150 kilometers south of Colombo, Mirissa sits just four meters above sea level and is famed for its serene beaches and vibrant nightlife. Unlike the busier coastal resorts, Mirissa offers a more secluded feel, with two beautiful sandy bays divided by a rocky island accessible at low tide. The town is dotted with cozy bars and surf rentals, but its true highlight is the thrilling whale-watching tours available from May to November. During your visit, explore attractions such as the stunning Coconut Tree Hill, the hidden gem of Secret Beach, and embark on an unforgettable whale-watching adventure.

Overnight stay at a hotel in Mirissa.

​

After breakfast, set out to explore the scenic coastal city of Bentota, located on the southern shores of Sri Lanka. Known for its natural beauty and birdlife, Bentota is a haven for nature lovers, with opportunities to spot rare species such as the Indian Heron and various migratory birds along the riverbanks.

Begin your day with a Madu River boat ride, a serene journey through lush mangroves and vibrant wetlands. This ecological treasure is home to a wide variety of flora and fauna, including 70 species of fish, 50 types of butterflies, and over 100 species of birds—making it a paradise for wildlife and bird enthusiasts.

Next, visit the Kosgoda Turtle Hatchery, a vital conservation project dedicated to protecting Sri Lanka’s endangered sea turtles. Learn about the life cycle of turtles and witness the heartwarming sight of baby turtles being cared for before they are released into the sea.

In the evening, enjoy some thrilling water sports activities in Bentota, such as jet skiing, banana boat rides, or windsurfing, perfect for adventure seekers.

Dinner and overnight stay in Bentota.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

We have been accomodated in premium Hotels and Cruise and moreover liasoning of the tour itinerary in skill hands by our tour operator Travelxploria made the tour Hassle Free, Memorable, Fascinating and Mind Blowing.

overview:
Best Sri Lanka Tour Package from India – Discover the Island of Serene Beauty and Culture

Discover the best Sri Lanka tour package from India, where lush tea gardens, golden beaches, ancient temples, and exotic wildlife come together in perfect harmony. With Travelxploria, your trusted Best Travel Agency and experienced International Tour Operator, explore Sri Lanka with expertly curated packages tailored for honeymooners, families, and group travelers from India. For more diverse travel options, check out our wide range of Tour Package India.
Curated by our certified travel consultants with over a decade of experience in South Asia tourism.
Whether you're seeking a SriLanka tour package from Chennai, a relaxing SriLanka tour package from Bangalore, or a scenic SriLanka tour package from Kochi, we have the perfect itinerary waiting for you.

Why Choose a Sri Lanka Tour Package from India?

Proximity, Visa-Free Entry, and Affordable Travel
Sri Lanka is just a short flight away from major Indian cities and offers visa-free entry for Indian citizens (source). It’s an ideal destination for a quick international getaway that won’t break the bank—especially with our
SriLanka tour package from Kerala, Mumbai, or Kolkata.
Pro Tip: Flights from Chennai to Colombo take under 90 minutes, making Sri Lanka one of the most accessible international destinations for Indian travelers.
Rich Heritage, Pristine Beaches, and Scenic Hill Stations
The country boasts a stunning mix of cultural treasures, serene beaches, and misty hills. With our best Sri Lanka tour package from India, you'll explore ancient temples, unwind on palm-fringed shores, and trek through the cool mountain air in tea country.
Must-see heritage sites include the Sigiriya Rock Fortress and the Sacred City of Anuradhapura.
Perfect for Honeymooners, Families, and Solo Explorers
Whether it’s a SriLanka tour package for couple, a family Sri Lanka tour package from India, or a solo escape, our itineraries suit every kind of traveler. You’ll find adventure, relaxation, and cultural immersion all in one trip. For honeymooners, explore our special honeymoon packages and for solo travelers, see our solo travel packages.

Top Sri Lanka Tour Packages from India
5 to 7 Days Sri Lanka Tour Package from India – Kandy, Colombo & Nuwara Eliya
Experience the highlights of Sri Lanka in under a week. Start in Colombo, visit the sacred city of Kandy, and enjoy the British colonial charm of Nuwara Eliya—the perfect SriLanka tour package from Kolkata or Chennai.
Sample Itinerary:
Day 1: Arrive Colombo – City Tour
Day 2: Transfer to Kandy – Visit Temple of the Tooth
Day 3: Kandy to Nuwara Eliya – Tea Estate Visit
Day 4: Ella Trekking – Scenic Train Ride
Day 5: Colombo Shopping + Departure
Sri Lanka Honeymoon Packages for Indian Couples
Our romantic SriLanka tour package for couple features candle-lit dinners, beachside resorts, and serene hill stations. Book a Sri Lanka honeymoon package from India at best price, designed with privacy, luxury, and unforgettable experiences.
Resort Recommendation: Jetwing Lighthouse in Galle or 98 Acres in Ella are ideal for honeymooners.

Budget-Friendly Sri Lanka Holiday Packages from India
Travel affordably without compromising on quality. Our packages—from SriLanka tour package from Mumbai to Kochi—include flights, transfers, and curated stays, all at competitive rates.
Tip: Book during the shoulder seasons (April or October) for the lowest prices and fewer crowds.
Customized Sri Lanka Itineraries for Families and Small Groups
From wildlife safaris to cultural tours, we offer personalized plans perfect for a family Sri Lanka tour package from India or guided Sri Lanka group tour packages from India with multilingual guides and flexible options.
Customization includes dietary preferences, elderly-friendly itineraries, and activity adjustments for kids. For more family travel options, see our dedicated family tour packages.

Must-Visit Places in Sri Lanka Covered in Tour Packages

Colombo – Colonial Architecture, Shopping & Nightlife
Begin in Colombo, the vibrant capital that fuses modern malls and colonial-era buildings. Enjoy local cuisine, street markets, and a buzzing nightlife. Don’t miss Pettah Market and the Dutch Hospital Precinct.

Kandy – Temple of the Tooth and Cultural Shows
Kandy, Sri Lanka’s cultural hub, is home to the Temple of the Tooth Relic and traditional Kandyan dance performances. Don’t miss the Esala Perahera festival if you visit in July-August.

Nuwara Eliya – Tea Gardens and Mountain Views
Nicknamed “Little England,” this highland retreat offers lush tea plantations, cool weather, and scenic train rides—an ideal stop for nature lovers and honeymooners. Recommended: Visit Pedro Tea Estate and Gregory Lake.

Bentota – Beach Resorts and Water Sports
Located on the southwestern coast, Bentota is perfect for water sports, beach lounging, or romantic stays by the sea—ideal for couples booking a SriLanka tour package for couple.

Sigiriya & Dambulla – Ancient Rock Fortresses and Temples
Explore Sri Lanka’s ancient past at Sigiriya, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and the cave temples of Dambulla, rich with murals and Buddha statues. Tip: Climb early in the morning to avoid crowds and heat.

Inclusions in the Best Sri Lanka Tour Packages from India
Return Flights from Major Indian Cities
We offer all-inclusive packages with return airfare from Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai, Kolkata, Kerala, Kochi, and Bangalore. You can also explore other nearby destinations with our nepal tour package or broaden your trip with the norway tour package from india.

Hotel Accommodation with Daily Breakfast
Enjoy premium or boutique stays, including breakfast and other amenities tailored to your package. Hotel categories available: 3-star to 5-star and boutique eco-stays.

Private Transfers and Local Sightseeing Tours
Every best Sri Lanka tour package from India includes AC vehicle transfers, licensed guides, and curated sightseeing to key attractions.

Travel Insurance and Indian Language Tour Assistance (Optional)
Optional Indian-language support and travel insurance ensure a smooth and secure experience for families, couples, and groups.

Best Time to Visit Sri Lanka from India

December to April – Ideal Weather for the West and South Coast
Travel during this period to enjoy the sunshine and coastal escapes of Bentota, Mirissa, and Colombo.

May to September – Best for East Coast Beaches
Enjoy the calm beaches of Trincomalee and Arugam Bay with less rainfall and vibrant marine life.

Festival Seasons – Esala Perahera and Sinhala New Year
Time your trip around April (Sinhala New Year) or July-August (Kandy's Esala Perahera) to witness Sri Lanka's vibrant traditions.

Frequently Asked Questions About Sri Lanka Tour Packages from India

Do Indian citizens need a visa to visit Sri Lanka?
No. As of now, Indian passport holders can enter Sri Lanka visa-free (verify here), making it easier than ever to plan a quick international holiday.

What is the ideal duration for a Sri Lanka trip from India?
A 5 to 7-day itinerary is ideal to explore key cities like Colombo, Kandy, Nuwara Eliya, and Bentota. You can also extend your stay for wildlife safaris or deeper cultural immersion.

Is Sri Lanka a good honeymoon destination for Indian couples?
Absolutely. The Sri Lanka honeymoon package from India at best price includes romantic stays, private beach dinners, scenic train rides, and spa treatments designed for couples.

What are some top options for family-friendly Sri Lanka tours?
Our family Sri Lanka tour package from India offers kid-friendly hotels, guided nature tours, and cultural experiences like elephant spotting and temple visits. Add-on: Optional family rooms, vegetarian meal plans, and educational cultural stops.

Are there group tours available for Sri Lanka from India?
Yes, our guided Sri Lanka group tour packages from India are perfect for friends, senior travelers, or corporate retreats, offering shared experiences with expert local guides. Features: Group leader, shared coach transport, and fixed departure dates.

How to Book the Best Sri Lanka Tour Package from India

 Trusted Indian Travel Agencies and Online Booking Sites
Book your trip via Travelxploria, recognized as one of the Best Tour Operators in India, known for curated itineraries, transparency, and value. Check customer reviews and testimonials on Google & Trustpilot.

 Tips for Choosing the Right Sri Lanka Itinerary
Choose based on your travel style—beach, heritage, wildlife, or a mix. Consider the weather and duration when selecting from SriLanka tour package from Bangalore, Mumbai, or Kochi.

 What to Pack and Know Before You Travel
Pack light clothes, sun protection, modest attire for temples, and your passport. Respect local customs and try local dishes like hoppers and kottu roti for an authentic experience. Travel Tip: Carry a universal adapter, sunscreen, mosquito repellent, and Sri Lankan Rupees.
Explore Sri Lanka with Travelxploria—your journey to paradise starts with the best Sri Lanka tour package from India.
